topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my screen, a jarring intrusion into the quiet hum of my evening. My stomach clenched, a cold knot tightening beneath my ribs. It was a request. From Mark. Mark, the architect of scraped knees and whispered cruelties in the third grade. My fingers hovered over the 'Accept' button, trembling slightly. A wave of nausea washed over me; the memory of his sneering face was vivid, sharp. Why now? After all this time, the silence between us had been a fragile, hard-won peace. I stared at the profile picture—a forced, overly confident grin—and felt a sudden, desperate urge to delete the entire application. My breath hitched, shallow and fast.

[story 2]

I hadn't expected the ping. It landed in my inbox like a dropped stone in still water, sending ripples of unease across my calm surface. When I clicked it open, the sight of his name made my jaw tighten. It wasn't a shock so much as a slow, creeping dread. I leaned back in my chair, the worn fabric digging into my spine. Every muscle in my neck felt suddenly rigid. *What does he want?* The question echoed in the hollow space where my usual composure resided. I kept re-reading the message, the carefully chosen, almost mocking tone of his words grating against my nerves. I traced the outline of the screen with my thumb, feeling a distinct pressure building behind my eyes, a heavy weight settling on my chest.

[story 3]

The phone vibrated insistently, pulling me from the flow of my work. It was a notification, and the familiar sight of his name made my hands shake, the slight tremor making the keyboard feel alien beneath my fingertips. I didn't move for a long moment, just staring at the glowing rectangle. A sudden, hot flush crept up my neck, staining my cheeks. I could feel the blood pounding in my ears, loud and insistent. This was unwelcome. It felt like stepping back onto uneven ground after a long, careful walk. I swallowed hard, the dryness in my throat making the action difficult. I finally tapped the screen, the sound of the connection establishing itself seeming deafening in the sudden stillness of the room.
